title: Trauma + EMDR Therapy
subtitle: You've done an incredible job surviving. Now it's time to start living.

sections.title: You may be dealing with the effects of trauma if...
sections.body:
Many adults experience anxiety not as panic, but as constant mental noise. 

* You're on edge and tend to emotionally overreact to what others might consider small stressors.
* Whether you're irritable and lashing out or numb and shut down, you just want to feel "normal" again.
* Hypervigilance - the constant scanning for safety/danger signals - keeps you anxious and disconnected.
* You experience flashbacks and try to avoid triggering people or situations.
* Being critical and mistrustful of yourself and others reinforces your need to withdraw and keep your distance.
* Relationships feel unstable. People walk on eggshells around you.
* A part of you wants closeness, and another fears it.
* You struggle to feel pleasure. Guilt, shame, and worthlessness weigh heavy on you.

**Reclaim your peace and sense of control.**

sections.title: What happened to you doesn't define you.
sections.body:
The word trauma means "wound." While I don't subscribe to the idea that awful things happen for a reason, I find hope and comfort in the fact that wounds heal. You can't go back and change what happened to you, but you can heal from it in a way that allows you to look back on it with emotional distance instead of staying stuck being plagued by the long-term impacts.

You deserve to be whole again. You have the capacity. You don't have to do this alone.

sections.title: EMDR Therapy in Leesburg, VA
sections.body:
Eye Movement Desensitization and Reprocessing (EMDR) is a structured, evidence-based treatment modality recommended by global organizations including the World Health Organization (WHO) and the Department of Defense.

It's a therapeutic approach designed to decrease the emotional charge of traumatic experiences and link them to positive memory networks. By activating both hemispheres of the brain, EMDR allows you to reprocess distressing memories so they feel resolved.

sections.title: How do I know if my experience qualifies as trauma?
sections.body: Trauma is, unfortunately, ubiquitous. It isn isn’t only a matter of what happened to you, but how you managed and what happened after. Trauma happens when something is so upsetting our nervous system gets overwhelmed and we are pushed beyond our internal resources.

sections.title: Do I have to share sensitive or upsetting details?
sections.body: In short, no. EMDR therapy allows you to desensitize and reprocess traumatic experience(s) without having to share in any explicit detail.

sections.title: How is EMDR different than talk therapy?
sections.body: EMDR is considered a bottom-up treatment approach, meaning we focus on helping you regulate and adjust your responses by tapping into your body’s nervous system. This is different than traditional talk therapy’s top-down approach which focuses on your thoughts.

sections.title: Imagine being able to:
items: Feel more in control, less reactive, and better able to regulate your emotions
Develop a greater sense of trust in yourself and others
Better understand your thoughts, feelings, and behaviors
Believe in your ability to handle future challenges
Find a greater sense of purpose and meaning in your life
